title: What has my body carried this year
subtitle: A journal prompt for acknowledging the physical weight of the year
excerpt: A journal prompt for reflecting on what the body has carried in the past year. Stress, illness, loss, change. Most people track what happened without noting where it landed in the body.

practice.whenToUse: At the end of a hard year, after a period of physical or emotional strain, or when you feel disconnected from your body

content.text: The body records things the mind moves past. Stress, grief, and sustained effort all land somewhere physical. This prompt asks you to look at that ledger.
Scan your body right now, top to bottom. Where is there tension, weight, or tiredness that you have been ignoring? Note it without trying to fix it.
What has happened this year that you processed in your head but maybe not in your body?
What has this year asked of your body? Include illness or injury if those apply, and the quieter costs too: interrupted sleep, held breath, the days you pushed through.
Where in your body did this year show up? What physical changes, symptoms, or sensations do you associate with this period?
What does your body need now that it has been waiting for? Write one thing you could give it this week.
